title: The Ziff Who Came To Dinner

jokes.text: I will not speculate on how hot teacher used to be
jokes.setup: Chalkboard punishment opening - Bart must write lines
jokes.punchline: The specific forbidden speculation about teacher's past attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons chalkboard gag with adult humor disguised as childish punishment - economy and character integration are strong

jokes.text: d-ohh! (Screams)
jokes.setup: Couch gag setup (inferred from scream following D'oh)
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation followed by physical comedy
jokes.explanation: Iconic catchphrase but routine execution - perfect character integration but low originality

jokes.text: The wild dingleberries? It's a movie version of a cartoon family you can see for free on tv. But they stretched out the plot and added a wildebeest from the hood.
jokes.setup: Homer describing a movie based on a cartoon
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on cartoon-to-movie adaptations with absurd 'wildebeest from the hood' addition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on The Simpsons Movie itself, with perfect absurdist escalation

jokes.text: Sorry, pally, the dingleberries are sold out... but, looking at you I'm sure your kids are used to disappointment.
jokes.punchline: Brutal personal insult based on Homer's appearance
jokes.explanation: Sharp, unexpected insult with perfect setup-punchline structure and economy

jokes.text: My big fat greek salad? Not a movie.
jokes.punchline: Revealed to literally be a menu item, not a movie parody
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection with perfect economy - parodies movie title while being literally something else

jokes.text: What would jesus view?
jokes.punchline: WWJD parody applied to movie selection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders character integration with clever religious parody wordplay

jokes.text: This ice cream's too cold! Oh, I cut myself on the cone!
jokes.setup: Senior citizens enjoying ice cream outing
jokes.punchline: Complaints about fundamental properties of ice cream
jokes.explanation: Absurdist elderly complaints that escalate from reasonable to impossible

jokes.text: Birthday?! (sirens blaring) (gasping)
jokes.punchline: Birthday mention triggers medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Dark elderly humor with good timing and economy

jokes.text: (Rapping): I say a birth, a day, a birth-a-dee-day! Now my good-time gang will blow you away! (Screaming) never leave the day room. Never leave the day room!
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate rap followed by traumatic elderly responses
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept with cringe execution - cultural collision humor

jokes.text: Never leave the day room. Never leave the day room!
jokes.punchline: Repeated mantra suggesting institutional trauma
jokes.explanation: Dark institutional humor with repetitive structure that emphasizes trauma

jokes.text: We'll just go to the furniture store and sit down.
jokes.punchline: Homer's pathetically low entertainment standards
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic with excellent economy - captures his simple pleasures

jokes.text: Good? I'm in it. I had a small speaking role. Yeah, I went to visit him and was banned from the set.
jokes.setup: Lenny's pride in being in the movie
jokes.punchline: Carl's immediate deflation with additional embarrassing detail
jokes.explanation: Good escalating structure with Carl's perfect deflation of Lenny's moment

jokes.text: The director saw my photo in a medical book.
jokes.punchline: Subverted - discovered for medical deformity, not talent
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of Hollywood cliché with perfect economy and dark humor

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, there are lots of kids in it. They fall victim to a vicious... well, b-b-but don't ruin it for me!
jokes.punchline: Reveals children are victims, then Homer's oblivious enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Homer's characteristic obliviousness to danger

jokes.text: Your buttons came from the trousers of a psychotic killer?
jokes.punchline: Specific, ridiculous origin story for the evil buttons
jokes.explanation: Absurdist horror movie parody with specific ridiculous detail

jokes.text: hey, I paid for this popcorn, and I'm eating it.
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical priorities during horror
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - prioritizing snacks over appropriate fear

jokes.text: Everything you see is make believe, although it is based on a true story-- some of which happened in this very theater.
jokes.setup: Homer reassuring Lisa about fictional movie
jokes.punchline: Escalates to claiming parts happened in their theater
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure - undermines his own reassurance with frightening implications

jokes.text: the buttons look like they're sewn to my eyes, but they're really held on with hot wax.
jokes.setup: Lenny explaining his movie role during scary scene
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate behind-the-scenes explanation ruining the illusion
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment but disrupts pacing - Lenny's obliviousness to timing

jokes.text: honey, can daddy rest his soda on your head? Mm-hmm. That's my girl.
jokes.punchline: Homer using scared Lisa as human cup holder
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting moment - selfish but affectionate, Lisa's resigned compliance

jokes.text: Homer, this is a rare lapse in judgment for you.
jokes.setup: Expected criticism of Homer's poor judgment
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic 'rare' when Homer constantly shows poor judgment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge sarcasm with excellent economy - understated but cutting

jokes.text: But scaring kids is good for them. It hardens them against future terrors like roofing and driveway scams.
jokes.setup: Homer defending scary movie for kids
jokes.punchline: Escalates to specific adult mundane fears
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from general child-rearing philosophy to hilariously specific adult anxieties

jokes.text: there's no boogie woogies or woolly bullies out there.
jokes.punchline: Uses dated dance/song references as monster names
jokes.explanation: Good Marge character moment - her generational references showing through

jokes.text: Oh, your nightlight's burned out, and the only bulbs we have left are these red ones.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to help scared Lisa
jokes.punchline: Solution makes room more ominous with red lighting
jokes.explanation: Solid visual gag setup - parental help making things worse

jokes.text: It was only a movie... filmed in vancouver... with donald sutherland as the priest who stopped believing...
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to rationalize away fear
jokes.punchline: Specific details make it feel more real and frightening
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Lisa's rationality backfiring as details make horror more real

jokes.text: I think you're on your own, toots.
jokes.setup: Lisa proposing they explore attic together
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately abandoning her with casual callousness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart selfishness with good economy and casual cruelty

jokes.text: And bart, I'll see you in hell, you booger-eating wuss.
jokes.setup: Lisa's touching final messages to family
jokes.punchline: Ends with specific, childish insult to Bart
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from touching to savage, perfect sibling dynamics

jokes.text: That's right! We all know!
jokes.punchline: Implies family-wide knowledge of Bart's disgusting habit
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of previous insult with family conspiracy element

jokes.text: Visit us on the web at lisathemovie.Com.
jokes.punchline: Sudden corporate promotional interruption
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about corporate tie-ins disrupting narrative flow

jokes.text: homie, that kojak is sexier than cannon and barnaby jones put together. I think he looks a little like you. (Laughs) who loves ya, baby?
jokes.setup: Marge comparing Homer to TV detectives
jokes.punchline: Homer adopting Kojak catchphrase after comparison
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with dated TV references and Homer's ego

jokes.text: You have the right to remain sexy. Anything you touch can and will be held against you in a court of sex. If you cannot afford a sex-torney--
jokes.punchline: Miranda rights with increasingly forced sexual puns
jokes.explanation: Decent concept but forced execution - 'sex-torney' feels lazy

jokes.text: Good. Can you play with him for half an hour?
jokes.setup: Kids report there's a ghost in the house
jokes.punchline: Parents treat supernatural threat like babysitting opportunity
jokes.explanation: Excellent parenting comedy - perfect Homer and Marge response prioritizing convenience over child safety

jokes.text: I did find this newsweek from 1986: 'Why america loves saddam hussein.'
jokes.setup: Homer finding old magazine in attic
jokes.punchline: Headline showing how political relationships change over time
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire with perfect timing - references US-Iraq relationship changes

jokes.text: hello, simpsons! Artie ziff! None other! I've been hiding in your attic, living off the moisture I can suck from the rafters.
jokes.punchline: Artie's grandiose self-introduction followed by pathetic living details
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie character reintroduction - ego contrasted with pathetic circumstances

jokes.text: I thought we killed him! No, we didn't. But I did delete him from my bulk e-mail list. No, you didn't.
jokes.punchline: Marge's correction reveals Homer's pathetic 'revenge' also failed
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with perfect deadpan correction from Marge

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. (Chuckles) that monkey got america through some tough times.
jokes.punchline: Homer's inflated importance of crude internet humor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer perspective on crude internet humor's cultural significance

jokes.text: I would stop, but I love my voice!
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself with narcissistic honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie narcissism with immediate self-contradiction

jokes.text: and I was partying with newt gingrich, janeane garofalo and scottie pippen.
jokes.punchline: Impossibly diverse celebrity combination from different worlds
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist celebrity combination capturing dot-com excess randomness

jokes.text: My stock will have a slight rebound in 2003!
jokes.setup: Artie desperately trying to retain investors
jokes.punchline: Pathetically distant and modest promise of recovery
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment with perfect character desperation

jokes.text: they even took my repo vans.
jokes.setup: Artie explaining how much he lost
jokes.punchline: Even repossession vehicles were repossessed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with perfect economy - recursive repossession concept

jokes.text: Not even a fresh remark. Except this one. Wowie wow, wow, wow! Zazooga!
jokes.punchline: Immediately makes inappropriate exclamations
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Artie can't help himself

jokes.text: With his brains and your musky smell, I've got a full male role model.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining benefit of having Artie around
jokes.punchline: Reduces Homer's contribution to just musky smell
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart cruelty with excellent structure - compliment/insult combo

jokes.text: He tried once, but he got confused and thought the book was real. He's still looking for that chocolate factory. It consumes him.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining why Homer doesn't read to her
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation leading to obsessive quest
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect Homer characterization and quotable ending

jokes.text: Yeah, but after the ice cream man cut up his credit card, he got a little depressed.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Artie isn't available to play
jokes.punchline: Reveals Artie's financial desperation in mundane situation
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag setup with appropriate character detail

jokes.text: All: Hello, handsome! Hello... (quietly): Losers.
jokes.punchline: Artie's immediate dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Artie's arrogance even in desperation

jokes.text: can yodeling cure cancer? Of course not.
jokes.punchline: Immediate dismissal of the premise he just promoted
jokes.explanation: Perfect news media parody with excellent economy and structure

jokes.text: I lost all me 'screw you' money. I'm very sorry to hear that, willie. Screw you!
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates the loss by having to use 'screw you' for free
jokes.explanation: Brilliant concept with perfect execution - 'screw you money' demonstrated literally

jokes.text: That's some good spelunking, lou. Mighty fine spelunking.
jokes.setup: Police searching for Artie in caves
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's inappropriate appreciation for cave exploration
jokes.explanation: Decent Wiggum character moment with misplaced priorities

jokes.text: Hot mamma, I'm livin' in flush-town, population artie!
jokes.punchline: Overly elaborate celebration of poker hand
jokes.explanation: Good Artie character moment but slightly overwrought delivery

jokes.text: would you accept 98% of the outstanding shares of ziffcorp stock? And the peanuts in your mouth. Very well. Achem! All of them. Achem!
jokes.setup: Artie offering stock as poker bet
jokes.punchline: Homer's additional demand for Artie's mouth peanuts
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Homer's petty additional demand

jokes.text: I always knew some day I'd be a C.O.D.
jokes.punchline: Confuses CEO with 'Cash On Delivery'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer malapropism with excellent character integration

jokes.text: This guy's the one what done the thing that why you're here for. I'm talkin' malfeasance here.
jokes.punchline: Garbled grammar followed by surprisingly sophisticated legal term
jokes.explanation: Great Barney character moment - drunk grammar mixed with legal vocabulary

jokes.text: Don't tell my kids I'm going to jail! Tell them I joined the blue man group! I'm the fat one!
jokes.setup: Homer's desperate request for cover story
jokes.punchline: Absurd alternative explanation that's equally embarrassing
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect Homer logic - absurd cover story

jokes.text: Plead the fifth amend-what? Refuse to answer on the grounds that I what? Inseminate myself?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to invoke Fifth Amendment
jokes.punchline: Confuses 'incriminate' with 'inseminate'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer malapropism with sexual confusion added

jokes.text: You, sir, are a moron. A mormon?! But I'm from earth!
jokes.punchline: Homer's mishearing and alien conspiracy response
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from simple mishearing to alien conspiracy theory

jokes.text: and she was so beautiful, but what man would want her now? She was hot. Wouldn't want her.
jokes.setup: Judge's metaphorical description of damaged America
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal, shallow response to hypothetical woman
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer missing metaphor for literal interpretation

jokes.text: Anti-semitism? No. Your problem is you never think of anyone besides yourself.
jokes.setup: Artie asking why no one likes him
jokes.punchline: Assumes anti-Semitism, corrected to actual selfishness
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Marge's honest assessment

jokes.text: (Can-can song playing): Artie, artie-artie, artie artie-artie, artie, artie-artie-art ziff-ziff-ziff-ziff ziff-ziff-ziff.
jokes.setup: Artie claiming he thinks of others besides himself
jokes.punchline: Elaborate musical number entirely about himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual contradiction of his claim - perfect character demonstration

jokes.text: If he's out, can I move back in? Oh, what do you think?! And she's the nice one.
jokes.setup: Homer asking to return after Artie is kicked out
jokes.punchline: Artie's comment about Marge being 'nice' after her anger
jokes.explanation: Homer's reasonable question contrasted with Artie's misreading of situation

jokes.text: If he's out, can I move back in? Oh, what do you think?! And she's the nice one.
jokes.setup: Homer seeing opportunity in Artie's banishment
jokes.punchline: Marge's rejection and Artie's observation about her being 'nice'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Homer's opportunism and family dynamics

jokes.text: Daddy's on fire. Daddy's not on fire. Daddy's on fire-- daddy's not on fire. Daddy's on fire... daddy's not on fire! (Whines) okay, fine, he is. (Squeals)
jokes.setup: Marge reading inappropriate children's book about prison
jokes.punchline: Maggie's distress forces Marge to give in to the violent narrative
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Marge's parental defeat to baby's preferences

jokes.text: (Imitating homer): Why, you little... (choking) (head bangs) aw, it's no use, son. By the time I get out of here, you'll be grown.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to cheer Homer with impression
jokes.punchline: Homer's depression about missing Bart's childhood
jokes.explanation: Touching moment with callback to running gag, good character work

jokes.text: Turn around, turn around turn around and you're a young man and you're too big to choke. (Sobbing)
jokes.punchline: Specifically mourns being unable to choke grown Bart
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - touching parental regret focused on violence

jokes.text: (Laughing) (coughing)
jokes.setup: Artie lecturing teens about smoking dangers
jokes.punchline: Girls laugh so hard at his advice they trigger smoking coughs
jokes.explanation: Good ironic visual gag - laughter immediately proving the point

jokes.text: Oh... the hair is standing up on the back of my knees.
jokes.setup: Selma's reaction to hearing about Artie
jokes.punchline: Specific, unglamorous body part for attraction response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selma character moment with original anatomical comedy

jokes.text: Come on, short round, we're going back to my temple of doom.
jokes.punchline: Indiana Jones reference applied to her apartment/bedroom
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference with sexual innuendo

jokes.text: they say that the love of a good woman can save any man. Except you, freak.
jokes.punchline: Patty's immediate cruel exception for Artie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Patty cruelty with good setup-punchline structure

jokes.text: I can't get my socks off. Aw, leave 'em on. I like a man with a little mystery. I'm done. My kind of man.
jokes.punchline: Selma's low standards and quick satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with both characters' romantic desperation

jokes.text: (Gasps) wonderful, glorious, magnificent. And you were pretty good, too.
jokes.punchline: Artie's narcissistic focus on his own performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie narcissism even in intimate moments

jokes.text: First, I'm going to read the paper, have a little nosh, and then I'm going to rinse out a few things, and then, finally, I will get an innocent man out of jail!
jokes.setup: Artie's moral awakening to help Homer
jokes.punchline: Prioritizes mundane activities before heroic action
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Artie's priorities even during redemption

jokes.text: Well, he can't break my heart, 'cause he kind of makes me sick. This could work.
jokes.punchline: Optimistic about relationship based on disgust
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Selma character insight with perfect economy

jokes.text: And I'm happy to listen to fm 95.3, home of boomer and the diz! Bart! Chill out, mom. They gave me this hat.
jokes.punchline: Marge's obvious product placement, Bart's mercenary honesty
jokes.explanation: Good media satire with perfect character responses

jokes.text: You can either steal a car or kick me in the crotch. (Grunts): No one ever chooses the car.
jokes.setup: Wiggum offering Homer choice of crimes
jokes.punchline: Reveals everyone chooses to assault him personally
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with perfect Wiggum incompetence

jokes.text: You can either steal a car or kick me in the crotch. (Grunts): No one ever chooses the car.
jokes.setup: Chief offering Homer options for compensation
jokes.punchline: Police brutality is apparently more popular than car theft
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about violence being preferred over property crime

jokes.text: Oh, my god! Maggie, you're talking! Homer, I believe that's lisa. Oh, bart, what a man you've become.
jokes.punchline: Completely misidentifies each family member in sequence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Homer's characteristic confusion

jokes.text: All I needed was the love of a good woman, and since you're not interested, uh... I'm not! Merely checking, because I'm into that.
jokes.punchline: Still can't help hitting on her, then claims it was research
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie character consistency - can't resist even while being noble

jokes.text: Oh, smoker, eh? Well, I have ways of dealing with you. Squirt, squirt, squirt. Your lungs will thank me.
jokes.punchline: Continues his anti-smoking campaign in prison
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency with prison setting escalation

jokes.text: Oh, look, there's a whole bunch of you. Squirt, squirt, squirt. That's it, circle around me. Squirt, squirt, squirt, squirt, squirt, squirt. Oh, I'm going to need more water.
jokes.punchline: Overwhelmed by multiple smokers, realizes he's outgunned
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation showing Artie's inevitable defeat in prison
